# 08 — `AgentServer.state/1` ships the entire internal State struct to every caller

**Severity:** Medium
**Location:** `lib/jido/agent_server.ex` (`handle_call(:get_state, ...)`, `state/1`, `status/1`, `stream_status/2`), `lib/jido/await.ex` (`lookup_child_pid/2`, `get_children/1`, `alive?/1`)

## Problem

`handle_call(:get_state, ...)` replies with the full `%State{}`: the agent struct (which
can hold large user state and thread history), the directive `:queue`, up to 500 debug
events, children/cron maps, the compiled signal router, completion waiters, etc.

Everything is built on this one call:

- `status/1` fetches the whole State just to compute a snapshot from `state.agent`.
- `stream_status/2` polls it every 100ms by default.
- `Jido.Await.child/4` polls it every **50ms** just to read one child pid.
- `Await.alive?/1` does a full state copy to answer a boolean (and is advertised as
  "safe to interact" — a `Process.alive?` would do).

Each call copies the entire term across the heap boundary. For agents whose appeal is
holding rich state (the whole point of the framework), routine monitoring becomes
proportional to state size. It is also an encapsulation leak: the moduledoc itself warns
that `State` internals "may change without notice," yet `state/1` is the documented
completion-detection mechanism ("External code polls AgentServer.state/1"), so users
*will* couple to internals.

## Recommendation

- Add cheap, purpose-built calls: `:get_status` (already half-exists via `Status`),
  `:get_child_pid, tag`, `:queue_length`. Reply with small maps, not the State struct.
- Re-point `Await`, `status/1`, and `stream_status/2` at those.
- Keep `state/1` as an explicitly-debug API (or move it behind `Jido.Debug`).
- For very hot read paths (status polling dashboards), consider publishing the snapshot
  into the Registry value or an ETS table on transition, so reads don't touch the
  GenServer at all — this also keeps monitoring working while the agent is busy
  (see finding 03).
title: F08: `AgentServer.state/1` ships the entire internal State struct to every caller
